genre_refs: Battle of New Orleans (Edward Percy Moran painting); War of 1812 history
description: A reproduction of the early-20th-century history painting 'The Battle of New Orleans' by Edward Percy Moran (identified in the tweet text). The dramatic oil painting depicts the January 1815 battle: General Andrew Jackson stands atop an earthwork rampart at right, sword raised, in a dark blue uniform, the Stars and Stripes flying behind him; American defenders fire muskets from the parapet. At left and center, red-coated British soldiers and kilted Scottish Highland infantry surge up scaling ladders against the fortifications, some falling wounded; a Union Jack and a fallen drum are visible amid the chaos; smoke and a stormy sky fill the background, with ships faintly on the water at far left. The image is a straight reproduction of a fine-art painting with NO added graphic-design elements, overlaid text, typography, slogans, or agency logo on the image itself (the OCR is noise from the painting). The Department of Labor used it as a patriotic/heritage illustration with a caption invoking the 'Rugged American Spirit' and 'Remember who you are, American.' Because it is an unmodified artwork reproduction rather than an agency-designed graphic, marked is_designed=false. Target audience: patriotic/history-minded public.
notable_text: (no on-image text; the artwork is 'The Battle of New Orleans' by Edward Percy Moran per the tweet)
